performance_overview: Through 21 sessions (inception 2026-06-01 through 2026-06-25) Bull has completed 7 trades: 3W/4L (43% win rate, avg +0.15%/trade). Cumulative: Bull -0.0612% vs SPY -3.52% (last known; SPY unavailable today due to TLS error), alpha +3.46 pts. Today (2026-06-25) was the 17th consecutive no-entry session — all 4 watchlist names (ES, MGM, AMD, PANW) gap-up trapped again: opened RSI 67-83, reversed to RSI 36-54 by midday. Cash preservation continues to outperform significantly.
best_signals: RSI 55-60 at entry: 2/2 wins (100%), avg +2.15% — the only consistently profitable bucket; enforce hard ceiling at RSI 60; n=2 (directionally clean, sample small)
avoid: RSI 60-65 at entry: 0/3 wins (0%), avg -1.06% — all overbought chase entries have lost; hard ceiling, no exceptions
RSI 65-75 at entry: 0/1 wins (0%), avg -0.61% — confirmed gap-up trap pattern; skip entirely; 5th+ consecutive instance of this failure mode in 2026-06-25 session
Gap-up opens with RSI >65: Zero qualifications in all sessions; any name opening RSI >65 is a hard skip regardless of structural setup quality
overnight_hold_insights: Zero overnight carries across all 21 sessions. The six-criteria gate (profitable + trailing-stop-active + above EMA-21 + no earnings within 2 days + sentiment not negative + 5% gap-risk cushion) has never been met simultaneously. Extended/thin candidate pool prevents qualified entries, let alone overnight candidates. Policy is sound — no change warranted.
recent_market_context: Last 5 sessions (2026-06-21 through 2026-06-25): SPY DOWN regime persists for 17th+ consecutive no-entry session. Consistent gap-up trap pattern throughout — watchlist names open RSI 65-83, reverse sharply by midday to RSI 36-55. Evening scan 2026-06-25 found only 4 survivors from 516 tickers (PANW, MGM, ES, IBKR) — extremely narrow leadership, confirming broad correction/consolidation. AMD dropped off watchlist (VCP/RS no longer qualifying). PANW and ES both closed above their prev_day_high today, raising gap-up trap risk for tomorrow. MGM remains the structural standout (VCP 0.679, vol dry 0.517) but is furthest from 52w high (8.5%). Market offers no qualified entries until RSI gap-up trap pattern breaks.
notes_for_next_session:
FOR PREMARKET AGENT (2026-06-26 open).

TOMORROW'S WATCHLIST (4 names, ranked by conviction):

  1. MGM ($47.21, prev_high $47.60) — HIGHEST CONVICTION STRUCTURE
     VCP 0.679 (best in group, cleanly <0.70 ideal), vol dry 0.517 (best in group), RS 1.133.

     Concern: 8.49% from 52w high, furthest in group — momentum needed.
     Entry if: RSI 55-60 at open, price approaching $47.60, rel_vol >=1.3, ATR% >=0.30.

     RS 1.170 (best in group), vol dry 0.694. VCP 0.769 (borderline).

     ⚠️ Displayed RS 1.074 appears below the 1.10 filter threshold (rounding discrepancy in scan).

HARD RULES FOR TOMORROW:
  1. RSI ≤ 60 at open — no exceptions. Skip any name with RSI >60 regardless of structure.
  2. rel_vol >= 1.3 AND ATR% >= 0.30 required before any entry.
  3. Verify SPY regime (EMA-9 vs EMA-21) — DOWN regime limits max positions to 5.
  4. PANW and ES closed above prev_day_high today — assume gap-up risk; RSI gate is the critical filter.
  5. MGM is the only name where a clean breakout (close <prev_high, RSI 55-60) is possible tomorrow.
